transcript.text: Hey, how's it going?
Good, how are you?
Well, yeah, crazy busy over there as usual, I'm assuming.
Yeah, I mean, we just need to get some people hired, you know, as you can imagine.
Yeah, for sure, it's all good when you're small and you're just starting out and just, like, building a team and, like, you know, you're managing everything and juggling all the hats.
And then once sales get past a certain point, and you're doing, like, 10 different roles, it's not manageable long-term, for sure.
How are you doing?
I'm okay, yeah, yeah, it's just us today, Megan had a bit of a family emergency, so she couldn't make it to the call, but, yeah, everything's going.
are doing?
How doing?
Well, equally busy over here, but busy is good.
I always tell myself.
And yeah, I like I would much prefer a fast paced, a fast paced day rather than just like, you know, slogs.
I've done that, too.
And the day just goes by.
Yeah, it's more about like finding enough hours in the day to like get everything done.
But that's that's a good thing.
But yeah, we can just quickly go through some stuff and I can probably get you some time back here.
Megan, not here.
There's not like a ton to cover.
But yeah, everything's perfect performance wise going well.
I'm really so like, yeah.
OK, so this past week, yeah, we've jumped another like huge jump in impressions.
One thing to mention about this and why I'm particularly so impressed is like we're seeing impression drop across like a lot of accounts.
And this is like an Internet wide thing.
This isn't.
This is something going on with Google right now that, like, people are still trying to figure out.
So we have, like, quite a few accounts, people talking about impression drops over the past, like, say, three weeks or so.
Like, there's a couple things that happened.
Like, Google limited how many, like, page results are showing up.
Like, that changed.
That shouldn't really be related.
But there's something going on that people are still trying to figure out.
However, it's not affecting discerns.
So, and there are, like, places where I'm seeing it's not, like, on the accounts that I'm working on where it's not.
But then there's ones where there's, like, oh, yeah, there's, like, considerable impression drops over the past, you know, few weeks.
So all that to say, I'm pretty stoked about that because, yeah, we have some accounts where it's just, like, pretty dramatic drops.
But then yet clicks aren't necessarily lost there.
So, you know, it's, like, CTR is good and all that kind of stuff.
But it's just, like, weird stuff is going on right now.
So,
But not here, which is pretty rad, I think.
So, yeah, looking at the site overall, like, click's not super up, like, what is that, like, 13 up on the site overall, week over week.
You know, pretty modest growth, still dropping off on weekends, pretty normal.
But then we have average position climbing a pretty great amount, which is fantastic.
And, yeah, the CTR, they're just, like, quality, like, 0.1% is, like, big impression growth, clicks not catching up as much.
That's going to affect where the CTR is sitting.
Same thing with the blog performance overall, nice big jump over this past week in impressions, like, 29% clicks.
You know, obviously more modest seven clicks, not a huge deal.
I did round up sort of the top pages this past week over week as well, which, like, we're seeing, you were asking last week, it's, like, Washington, Oklahoma.
Oklahoma, that main franchise tax information page is kind of like the standard one that seems to be always kind of there, which makes sense, like sort of like top of tier page.
But right now it's like Oklahoma and Washington.
So I don't know if you have any insight into that or if that makes sense to you at all.
But yeah, we do see some impression drops on like a couple of those, like that main franchise tax information page did drop a little bit over last week.
But I mean, it's going to fluctuate up and down each week.
So yeah, those are the two states that are ranking the highest.
I also gave a little roundup about like LLM referrals.
We talked about that a little bit last week.
We will dive into this a little bit more with Scrunch in the coming weeks as well to give like a bit more granular insight.
But I wanted to just kind of talk about the top referred pages over the past week, as well as like I looked at longer term, like since June, just out of curiosity.
So.
So.
So this is from, this is taken from the Looker dashboard.
So you can check this out as well, like anytime you want, but different kind of pages over the past week that are popping up, like different states, which is interesting.
And then if we look at the longer term, like over, like since June 1st, we've got kind of like a mixed bag.
So, you know, it's just going to depend what people are searching for.
I don't know if we're going to see like, oh, these are the top pages that are like consistently like ranking, like getting citations from LLMs.
It's just going to be like, you know, possibly related to timeline with, you know, dates and deadlines and that kind of thing.
But I'm pretty sure we're going to see a pretty mixed bag because it's going to be like, who's searching for what state and what they're on, like what question are they searching for.
But with Scrunch, we can dive a bit more into like what actual, you know, queries are driving these kind of referrals and what people are actually typing in to make sure that we are.
We're always giving them the information they need, but yeah, not like a ton of referrals from any of the LMs right now, but yeah, I expect that to kind of increase over time anyway.

Yeah, the only other thing I had to cover, or Megan was going to bring up actually, was talking a bit about refreshes and how we kind of wanted to handle that, and if you had any thoughts around that.

I noticed in Airtable, there's like a hold section we have, which I think goes back to carry, and I believe the pages in hold, you can look at them when you have time, are like previous pages that like predate us, that are your pages from before, that we, I think, carry it, and you know, we all had talked about like possibly looking at those first for refresh, because those are like the oldest.
You know, we're looking at kind of like three months lifespan on like traffic kind of peaking, but I think you're, you're, you're going to be.
A lot different because like you have consistent reason for like, you know, it's evergreen, evergreen content there.

But I think we do have to kind of think about like how often we want to check pricing and deadline, you know, like all these kind of things just to make sure that we're kind of rolling, like have a rolling refresh cadence as well.
So, yeah, just wondering if had any thoughts about that and like how you wanted to approach that before we just, because otherwise we'll just like kind of do our, our, what we normally do.
Where are the on hold ones?
They're in Airtable.
If you scroll down towards the bottom, it's like a black, let me move my screen so I can see what I'm doing here.
Let's see.
I believe it's a black tab.
Looking at them yesterday, so I know they're here.
Yeah, so they're, like, at the bottom, if you collapse all the fields, they're, do you see?
Yeah, I kind of had it, like, displayed, I had it filtered out, so it probably was harder to see.

So, yeah, these were put in here, like, pre-us, I think Carrie popped them in here, just as, like, these are kind of, like, the things we should look at, and then there's some other, like, there's other pieces in here that are not related to refresh, that are, like, duplicates and things like that, that we just have to filter out.
Yeah, so, I mean, we could prioritize these basically whenever you want.
The question is, well, my main concern, at least with the franchise tax specific articles, is that we reference those from our product.
So, like, let's say that you have Illinois franchise taxes due, we'll actually say, like, read disordance guidance, and it directs the product to that.
So, they need to be slightly different in approach, which is, like...
Highly specific, concise, explanatory, no additional information whatsoever.
The purpose is not SEO, it's like providing information to our...
Informational, yeah, yeah, yeah.
Getting them to answer their question.
So would you say that the structure on there as now is what we want to keep?
It's basically just like fact-checking, basically.
Ideally, obviously I'm like open to, you know, suggestion if you feel like there's sections that could be helpful here to add.
Yeah, we just don't want to like add too much fluff or like change it up too much.
Yeah, that makes sense.
I think from our perspective, like what we're most concerned about is if they're older, like, you know, how quickly does pricing change?
How quickly does like, I don't imagine like the deadlines and days change as fast, but like just is there and links, you know, has like, you know, as, as...
As government sites like reroute things and change things around, like are there dead links, any of those kind of things.
But I don't really see the need if it's like a page that's performing well, that's like important to be linking to the structurally change it.
You know, I think it's more of like a fact checking mission.
But yeah, we can like decide how we want to do it.
Like right now we're doing like 26 pages a week.
Like we can, we can like put in like, let's do like five of those pages a week as refreshes and roll through these kind of like, you know, oldest to newest kind of cadence.
That's kind of how like I would approach it, I think.
And then just kind of, yeah, tell Adi that it's more of like, we're not kind of doing it from an SEO perspective.
Like it's already going to be fine there, but yeah.
Okay, sweet.
I will talk to Megan about it when she's back.
And then maybe we'll start factoring some of those in the next like, yeah, like with the Nexus pages and stuff.
Like I ideally like.

I'd to see as many of those out as possible, so I don't want to put in like too many refreshes, because I'd like to see all those ones live, but like maybe like five, five per week or something, or five or six per week to kind of roll them out slowly.
Sounds good.
Sweet.
Yeah, that's pretty much all I had on my end.
I just wanted to ask you about that and your thoughts.
Everything else is as per usual and going great, so yeah.
Was there anything you wanted to cover?

No, I mean, hopefully once I, I would say, you know, we're hiring two salespeople right now, and we're hiring a go-to-market engineer who's really going to be focused on like list building and outbound email automation and stuff, and I'm hoping once I have those people in seed, I can start to like think about this again more, but it seems like it's progressing fine, so I'm not super concerned with it.
Yeah, I mean, like, it's one of those things that's, it's kind of like a, you know, if you build it, they will come kind of situation, like, you know, it's like.
It's like we're doing the good content.
It's there.
It's fulfilling the needs of what people are looking for.
Clearly, it's ranking.
It's answering queries.
It's doing everything we wanted to do, which is great.
So it's like best case scenario.

As you get more time, if there's things that you want us to like dive more into or like if you're like, hey, we've got this like outbound campaign, like let's build some content around that, this particular campaign or event or like whatever.
Like we can do different types of content like depending, but otherwise we'll just like keep plugging along as usual until then and everything's going good.
So yeah.
Cool.
Sounds good.
Okay.
Awesome.
I'll talk to you soon and yeah.
Have a great rest of your week.
Thanks, Jen.
See ya.
Okay.
Ciao.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync between Discern and GrowthX to discuss content performance, strategy, and next steps.](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=248.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Discern's impressions significantly increased (+29% blog) despite industry-wide drops](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=344.0)
  - [Top performing content: Oklahoma & Washington state pages; LLM referrals diverse](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=470.0)
  - [Refresh strategy: prioritize older "on hold" pages, focus on fact-checking vs. restructuring](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=615.0)
  - [Discern hiring 2 salespeople & go-to-market engineer; content strategy on autopilot for now](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=927.0)

## Topics

### Content Performance Metrics

  - [Impressions: Large jump, bucking industry trend of drops](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=344.0)
  - [Clicks: +13% site-wide, +7% blog (modest growth)](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=424.0)
  - [Avg. position: Significant climb](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=436.0)
  - [CTR: Slight drop (0.1%) due to impression growth outpacing clicks](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=444.0)
  - [Top pages: Oklahoma & Washington state info, franchise tax pages](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=470.0)

### LLM Referrals

  - [Low volume currently, expected to increase](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=600.0)
  - [Diverse page referrals, no clear pattern](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=581.0)
  - [Short-term (past week) vs. long-term (since June) differences](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=554.0)
  - [Scrunch to provide more granular query insights in coming weeks](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=521.0)

### Content Refresh Strategy

  - [26 pages/week currently refreshed](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=859.0)
  - [New approach: 5-6 refreshes/week, prioritizing older "on hold" pages](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=859.0)
  - [Focus on fact-checking, updating pricing, deadlines, links](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=840.0)
  - [Maintain current structure for product-linked pages (e.g., franchise tax)](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=756.0)
  - [Balance refreshes with new Nexus page creation](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=899.0)

### Discern Business Updates

  - [Hiring 2 salespeople and a go-to-market engineer](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=927.0)
  - [Engineer to focus on list building and outbound email automation](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=927.0)
  - [Content strategy currently on autopilot, performing well](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=950.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Implement new refresh strategy: 5-6 older pages/week, focus on fact-checking](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=859.0)
  - [Continue monitoring LLM referrals, await Scrunch's detailed query analysis](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=521.0)
  - [Maintain current content production pace, emphasizing Nexus pages](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=975.0)
  - [Revisit content strategy once new hires are onboarded and settled](https://fathom.video/share/QNevFahtScxiTUbqZxYqDpwHgq1kjAKH?tab=summary&timestamp=999.0)

action_items.description: Inform Adi about refresh approach for franchise tax articles - maintain structure, focus on fact-checking, not SEO optimization.
Talk to Megan re incorporating ~5 refresh pages/week into content plan, starting w/ "hold" section in Airtable. Focus on fact-checking, dead links, pricing updates for franchise tax articles.
